[2021-01-31 16:20] sano712 (sano): Valuing units based on how "interesting" they are gets weird really fast though. For example Urban Sentry is an incredibly bland unit and I don't think people would generally want to remake it into something more unique. It's nice to have a vigilant splitter because it fills a certain niche in the game. 

I think it would be harmful if we added more trinket units such as pixie because eventually we'd get sets with 3+ more of them, but currently it's extremely rare for that to happen in b+8. As it stands, it's just a way of using excess blue in certain occasions. It will often be ignored in sets but I don't see that as significantly harmful to the game, to the point where we want to remove it or change it.

[2021-02-01 07:14] p0lari (Polari): it's easy to say in the abstract that you want some unusual thing to be occasionally viable, but in practice a first turn tech build is not going to have very little leeway to do anything but keep buying the unit you rushed the tech for, which means the build is going to be basically the same every time you go for it
[2021-02-01 07:14] p0lari (Polari): your phrasing for that kind of game was "isn't 100% competitive", the rest of us usually call them autowins and aren't big fans of those
[2021-02-01 07:15] p0lari (Polari): so if you want to see more of those, you definitely should be playing with units that aren't properly balanced
[2021-02-01 07:16] cooledcannon: what if it was autodraws?
[2021-02-03 07:15] pikachumemes. (Pikachu Memes): I don't think this is true. The successful t1 tech openings (conduit + vivid -> zemora, animus -> smorcus, blast + galvanis -> flamimus -> hellhounds + galvanis) are good in a large part because they have the ability to transition into other units, either due to econ generation or tech sinks. I don't think games like these are always autowins, (since you're not just spamming the same unit over and over) but I'll admit there's little doubt they tend to favor one side over the other in high level play. Having said that natural conduit is still a part of the game so....

[2021-02-14 17:19] ryanfourpm: Has it ever been suggested that for ladder matches either player can request a switching of sides? If you don't accept the side switching the game is aborted, if you do then you play as the presumably worse side but you get something like 4x more rating on a win, 1x rating loss for a loss, as normal.

I think this suggestion would have the following outcomes, which are good:

1. Less tilt from losing to autowins as you can escape.
2. Less autowin sets will end up being played, because they will be aborted. And autowin sets are lame to play, obviously.
3. A theoretically perfect player can never lose rating, so the RNG factor on ladder is largely removed. Right now I assume there is some sort of weird rating cap because you can always roll red rush sets against much lower rated players.
4. It will result in a small number of high stakes games, which is spicy.

The only downsides I can think of is that elyot would have even more work to do and also if you were to try to cheat the rating system you can do it 4x as fast. Also, noobs might be confused about this but it isn't like noobs need this option anyways.
[2021-02-14 17:22] cooledcannon: the other downside is it is less elegant but still doesn't remove RNG completely, and as is is still largely relevant
[2021-02-14 17:23] cooledcannon: you still want to not roll a shitty side about as much as now

[2021-03-15 07:13] pikachumemes. (Pikachu Memes): I mean sure, galvani drone is the worst offender in this regard by a long shot, I'll admit that. I was just pointing out that the stated problems with galvani drone are by no means unique to the unit; it's just most noticeable with galvanis.
[2021-03-15 07:21] p0lari (Polari): it's not a "problem" that a unit is good in rush openings, it's a problem if it's so good that it eliminates counterplay and at worst trivialises the entire game because some specific line is so strong you can just execute it without considering what your opponent is doing and win
[2021-03-15 07:22] p0lari (Polari): that's like saying problems with old shadowfang are by no means unique to the unit because there are other red attackers too; it's just most noticeable with brokenly good game-warping units
[2021-03-15 07:25] p0lari (Polari): I'll give that hellhound does combo well with galvani in that replay, but even there that's a side note to the more general issue with using galvani in rushes, which is that early galvanis are supposed to be iffy because they can be picked off with an early attacker that would otherwise run into an absorber, but if you use them to enable an otherwise unreasonably fast opening, the opponent just can't get that attacker out
[2021-03-15 07:28] p0lari (Polari): if that opening was a turn slower, your galvanis would get picked off and you'd be left with no econ and most of your attack getting absorbed, and you'd be back to having a whole bunch of engis not getting any value out of energy nor soak
[2021-03-15 07:29] p0lari (Polari): note that putting all of this together requires flanimus to be in the set too, making this a three-unit combination
[2021-03-15 07:30] p0lari (Polari): so yeah if stars align the hellhound combo can be real, but I challenge you to find a set where galvani+sentinel specifically are any kind of a problem

I was just pointing out that the stated problems with galvani drone are by no means unique to the unit; it's just most noticeable with galvanis.

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: it's not a "problem" that a unit is good in rush openings, it's a problem if it's so good that it eliminates counterplay and at worst trivialises the entire game because some specific line is so strong you can just execute it without considering what your opponent is doing and win

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: that's like saying problems with old shadowfang are by no means unique to the unit because there are other red attackers too; it's just most noticeable with brokenly good game-warping units

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: I'll give that hellhound does combo well with galvani in that replay, but even there that's a side note to the more general issue with using galvani in rushes, which is that early galvanis are supposed to be iffy because they can be picked off with an early attacker that would otherwise run into an absorber, but if you use them to enable an otherwise unreasonably fast opening, the opponent just can't get that attacker out

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: if that opening was a turn slower, your galvanis would get picked off and you'd be left with no econ and most of your attack getting absorbed, and you'd be back to having a whole bunch of engis not getting any value out of energy nor soak

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: note that putting all of this together requires flanimus to be in the set too, making this a three-unit combination

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: so yeah if stars align the hellhound combo can be real, but I challenge you to find a set where galvani+sentinel specifically are any kind of a problem

[2022-03-04 11:33] torikunsnaps (Torikun): hey @Elyot I've grown curious about the way Prismata calibrates ELO right before it becomes visible once you hit Master rank
[2022-03-04 11:33] torikunsnaps (Torikun): how exactly were you able to achieve a calibrated ELO that scaled linearly with tier progress without requiring placement games once you hit Master rank?
[2022-03-04 11:38] elyot: the displayed rating and the underlying MMR are not the same, but as you go from tier 7 to 10, it slowly transitions until at tier 10, the displayed rating is the underlying MMR
[2022-03-04 11:38] torikunsnaps (Torikun): what does this mean?
[2022-03-04 11:39] elyot: MMR = matchmaking rating
[2022-03-04 11:39] torikunsnaps (Torikun): do you have a base elo starting at tier 7 that starts calibrating right then and there?
[2022-03-04 11:40] elyot: we're not quite using Elo, we have our own rating system that has a variance parameter as well (but it's different than systems like trueskill)
[2022-03-04 11:40] elyot: it actually is calculating your rating ever since you start playing
[2022-03-04 11:40] elyot: even playing bots, it's trying to estimate how good you are
[2022-03-04 11:41] elyot: the higher tiers actually loosely correspond to this rating, but lower tiers increase purely based on playing more
[2022-03-04 11:42] elyot: as you transition from lower tiers to higher tiers, it switches the weight of these two factors (how much you've played vs how good you are) in calculating your displayed skill (e.g. tier 8, 70%)
[2022-03-04 11:44] torikunsnaps (Torikun): do you think prismata's MMR system is something that could be applied to other games? your system seems to be very closely tied to this game's systems
[2022-03-04 11:45] torikunsnaps (Torikun): I'm only bringing up this topic because another game I play has an issue where it uses placement games in its highest arena to seed players in directly in its ELO system using games with extremely high K factor - so a lucky streak of placement games can put you in the top 100 for no effort
